summary: DeVault presents data showing Gmail is the largest source of spam registrations on SourceHut, outnumbering the second-largest offender by 5x. He argues that Gmail's dominant market position gives it the greatest responsibility to combat spam, not an excuse for the volume. Despite sending many abuse reports to Gmail, he has never received a follow-up or seen improvement. He stops short of blocking Gmail registrations only because 41% of SourceHut users rely on it, and urges technically proficient users to switch to independent email providers.
key_points: Gmail accounts for 5x more spam registrations on SourceHut than the next worst domain, with hard data from the database to prove it
Gmail's market dominance should mean greater responsibility to curtail spam, not a proportional excuse for producing more of it
Abuse reports to Gmail go unanswered and produce no improvement, unlike Amazon SES which actually responds
Email deliverability concerns about leaving Gmail are largely a myth if you set up DKIM and manage DNSBL listings properly
Gmail's reputation as a trusted provider means its spam often bypasses junk filters, making it more harmful than spam from lesser-known domains
money_quotes: 5× as many spam registrations on sourcehut are from gmail than from the second-largest offender.

One might argue that, because Gmail is the world's largest email provider, it's natural to expect that they would have the largest volume of spam simply because they have proportionally more users who might use it for spam. I would argue that this instead tells us that they have the largest responsibility to curtail spam on their platform.
I've forwarded many, many reports to abuse@gmail.com, but they've never followed up and the problem has not become any better.
I have had half a mind to block Gmail registrations on sourcehut outright, but about 41% of all registrations use Gmail.
It bears repeating that anyone with any level of technical expertise ought to know better than to use Gmail.
If you're worried about mail deliverability issues, don't be — it's more or less a myth in $CURRENTYEAR.
key_insight: Gmail's dominance makes it both the largest source of spam and the least accountable for it, inverting the expected relationship between market power and responsibility.
